This classification is based on the current understanding of plant taxonomy. Calceolaria debilissima is a species of flowering plant in the genus Calceolaria. intro: Calceolaria debilissima, also known as the weak pocketbook, is a plant species belonging to the Calceolaria genus, native to the Andes mountain range in South America. The health benefits of Calceolaria debilissima include its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various health conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, including calceolariin, which have been shown to exhibit potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The most common herbal preparations made with Calceolaria debilissima include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are traditionally used in folk medicine to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders.
